skill.name: conference-tweet-generator
skill.description: Generates academic conference tweets and summaries by filtering abstracts, translating content, and creating engaging titles. Use when you need to process conference abstracts into social media content.
skill.summary: Processes academic conference abstracts to generate structured tweets and summaries ready for social media posting.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 40 potential issues across 4 files, but all findings are false positives upon evaluation. The 22 shell backtick execution findings are within markdown code blocks in SKILL.md showing example bash commands as documentation only. The 8 weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false detections in plain text documentation and a drug name reference dictionary. The 3 system reconnaissance findings are in a JSON audit report file, not in executable code. The only executable script (validate_skill.py) is a minimal argparse-based helper. The drug_dictionary.md contains a single long line of drug name pairs which triggered an obfuscation heuristic, but the content is a straightforward data reference. No prompt injection, malicious intent, or business logic flaws were identified.

content.user_title: Generate Conference Tweets from Academic Abstracts
content.value_statement: Researchers and science communicators need an efficient way to convert conference abstracts into social media content. This skill filters abstracts, translates drug names, and generates structured tweets and summaries ready for posting.
actual_capabilities: Filter academic conference abstracts by topic relevance
Translate drug and compound names using a reference dictionary
Generate 3 high-impact tweet titles per abstract following a consistent format
Create structured summaries with Background, Methods, Results, and Conclusion sections
Search for conference background information to provide accurate meeting context
limitations: Requires pre-collected conference abstracts as input material
Cannot attend conferences or access real-time presentation data
Drug name translations depend on the completeness of the provided reference dictionary
Tweet quality depends on the detail and clarity of source abstracts

use_cases.title: Sharing conference findings on social media
use_cases.description: A researcher returns from a medical conference and uses this skill to generate engaging social media posts from the abstracts they collected.

use_cases.title: Creating digestible science summaries
use_cases.description: A science writer processes multiple conference abstracts to create clear social media content for a general audience.

use_cases.title: Promoting conference sessions
use_cases.description: A conference organizer generates promotional tweets for each session using the submitted abstracts and program details.

prompt_templates.title: Basic tweet from abstract
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a tweet about this abstract: [paste abstract]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick tweet from a single abstract

prompt_templates.title: Topic-based abstract filtering
prompt_templates.prompt: Filter these conference abstracts for studies related to [topic]. Generate one tweet for each relevant abstract.

prompt_templates.title: Drug translation with dictionary
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the drug dictionary at references/drug_dictionary.md to translate drug names in these abstracts. Then generate tweets with correct drug name translations.

prompt_templates.title: Full conference report workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for information about [Conference Name Year]. Filter the provided abstracts by [topic]. For each abstract, extract key content, translate drug names, and generate 3 tweet titles. Create a meeting introduction based on search results.

output_examples.input: Abstract describing a Phase 3 trial of an immunotherapy drug for advanced lung cancer with response rate data.
output_examples.output: ASCO 2025 | Pembrolizumab shows 43% response rate in advanced lung cancer patients. Key finding: improved progression-free survival by 8.2 months compared to standard therapy. #ASCO2025 #LungCancer

output_examples.input: Conference abstract: Phase 3 trial results for a targeted therapy in HER2-positive breast cancer patients.
output_examples.output: ASCO 2025 | BREAKING: Trastuzumab deruxtecan achieves 78% overall response rate in HER2-positive breast cancer. Median progression-free survival not yet reached at 18-month follow-up. #ASCO2025 #BreastCancer

output_examples.input: Collection of 5 abstracts from the European Society of Cardiology 2025 annual meeting.
output_examples.output: ESC 2025 SUMMARY: New heart failure therapies dominate this year. Top highlights include a novel SGLT2 inhibitor showing 31% mortality reduction and a gene therapy for inherited cardiomyopathy entering Phase 2 trials. Full thread below. #ESC2025 #Cardiology

best_practices: Search for conference background information using web search before generating tweets to ensure accuracy of event details
Use the drug translation dictionary consistently to maintain correct drug name references across all tweets
Verify generated tweet content against source abstracts to prevent hallucination of numerical results or claims
anti_patterns: Generating tweets without searching for conference details first, which leads to fabricated or incorrect event information
Skipping the drug dictionary step and guessing drug name translations, causing inconsistent terminology
Creating tweets from incomplete or vague abstracts without requesting clarification on missing key data

faq.question: What input does this skill need to work?
faq.answer: The skill needs conference abstracts as input. You can provide them directly in your prompt.

faq.question: Does this skill attend conferences in real time?
faq.answer: No. The skill processes abstracts that you provide and searches the web for conference background information.

faq.question: How are drug names translated in the output?
faq.answer: Drug names are translated using a reference dictionary included in the skill package at references/drug_dictionary.md.

faq.question: Can this skill be used for any academic conference?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill works with any academic conference where you have access to abstracts or program details.

faq.question: Does this skill post tweets automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates tweet text. You must post the content manually to your social media accounts.

faq.question: How many tweets can this skill generate at once?
faq.answer: The skill can process multiple abstracts in a single session. Each abstract generates up to 3 tweet titles and one structured summary.
